key_highlights: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh faces stagflationary 'doom loop' as war-driven inflation persists and bond yields stay elevated.
Iran conflict remains a dominant macro risk, with Hormuz LNG tanker resuming operations but no final deal yet; US blockade redirected 100 vessels.
Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda spreads, with US aid down 99%, adding a humanitarian and potential travel disruption risk.
Equity markets show resilience: S&P 500 and Nasdaq score weekly wins, Dow logs record high, but Nvidia misses Friday's rally.
AI infrastructure and cybersecurity stocks remain high-growth themes; ARM, Vertiv, and AI energy stocks near buy points.
Consumer discretionary faces headwinds: retail chain closures, high gas prices, and rising food costs pressure spending.
Airline sector active: Allegiant acquires Sun Country, JetBlue partners with United, Ryanair reports record profits.
Space and defense: SpaceX Starship test success, Space Force awards $437M contract, L3Harris ramps fiber-optic tethers.
Energy: Kosmos Energy record output, Cameco valuation check after pullback, LNG tanker exits Hormuz for first time since war began.
Emerging markets face resurgent political risk, derailing rallies; Bangladesh launches $5B growth fund.
Big Tech: Meta moves 7,000 staff into AI task force; Alibaba unveils AI chip; Cisco extends USGA partnership.
Healthcare: Eli Lilly positive Phase 3 obesity results, Merck endometrial cancer trial meets endpoints, Pfizer oncology partnerships raise valuation concerns.
Regulatory: CLARITY Act for Bitcoin has 70% odds; Trump's 3,711 trades point to multiple stock strategies.
Geopolitics: Japan-China brief talk at APEC, Hungary's new premier warns of budget skeletons, US-Iran talks show 'significant progress'.

sectors.trend: Strong momentum driven by AI demand, AGI CPU demand pushing ARM to all-time highs, and AI energy infrastructure stocks near buy points. Meta's internal AI task force expansion and Alibaba's new AI chip underscore sector investment.
sectors.implications: Investors should focus on companies with direct AI exposure (ARM, Vertiv, ASML) but be cautious of overvaluation; pullbacks in ARM may offer entry. AI energy plays (e.g., VRT) benefit from data center power needs.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Ryanair reports record profits (+40% PAT), Allegiant acquires Sun Country, JetBlue partners with United, but high gas prices and expensive summer travel weigh on consumer sentiment. Analysts remain bullish on LATAM, Copa, and SkyWest.
sectors.implications: Low-cost carriers (ULCC, RYAAY) and network carriers with strong loyalty programs (UAL, DAL) may outperform. Monitor fuel costs and Iran-related supply disruptions.

sectors.trend: War-driven inflation persists; Fed's favored gauge shows price pressures. Kosmos Energy reports record output and debt reduction. LNG tanker exits Hormuz for first time since war began. Gas prices rise as summer travel starts.
sectors.implications: Energy stocks (Kosmos, Frontline, Exxon) may benefit from elevated prices, but geopolitical resolution could reverse gains. LNG and tanker stocks (FRO) offer income but face headline risk.

sectors.trend: Positive trial results for Eli Lilly (obesity) and Merck (endometrial cancer). Ebola outbreak spreads in Congo/Uganda, US aid down 99%. Pfizer oncology partnerships raise valuation concerns. Biodesix upgraded on revenue assumptions.
sectors.implications: Obesity and oncology remain high-growth areas; LLY and MRK are core holdings. Ebola could pressure travel and healthcare stocks. Biodesix and Eton offer speculative upside.

sectors.trend: Retail chain closures (33-year-old chain), high food costs, and expensive summer travel pressure consumers. Decker's double beat questioned. Home Depot considered low-risk. Amazon Memorial Day sales live.
sectors.implications: Cautious on retail; focus on low-risk names (HD) and companies with strong brand loyalty (Deckers). Restaurant and travel-related stocks face margin compression.

sectors.trend: Space Force awards $437M to Viasat/SES. SpaceX Starship test success. L3Harris ramps fiber-optic tethers. RTX demonstrates self-healing communications. SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic IPOs test AI boom limits.
sectors.implications: Defense and space stocks (LHX, RTX, Viasat) benefit from government contracts. SpaceX IPO could be a major catalyst. Monitor geopolitical tensions for defense spending boosts.

sectors.trend: Verizon partners with Anthropic, AT&T launches eSim for travelers. Comcast and T-Mobile analyst views mixed. Warner Bros. Discovery PT raised by UBS. Netflix considered top communication stock.
sectors.implications: Telecoms with AI partnerships (VZ) may see growth. Media stocks (WBD, NFLX) face streaming competition but remain investable. T-Mobile (TMUS) is a defensive pick.

sectors.trend: Resurgent political risk derails rallies. Bangladesh launches $5B growth fund. China coal mine blast tests energy security. Japan-China brief talk at APEC. South Korea day traders get risky new tools.
sectors.implications: EM exposure should be hedged; Bangladesh fund may boost infrastructure. China energy and AI (Alibaba) remain key. Political risk in Hungary and Iran talks add uncertainty.

key_themes: Iran conflict and war-driven inflation dominate macro outlook
AI infrastructure and cybersecurity remain high-growth, but valuations are stretched
Consumer spending under pressure from high gas prices and food costs
Ebola outbreak adds humanitarian and potential travel disruption risk
New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh faces stagflationary 'doom loop'
Airline sector consolidation and record profits for some, but fuel costs a concern
Defense and space spending boosted by government contracts and geopolitical tensions
Emerging markets face political risk headwinds
Regulatory clarity for Bitcoin (CLARITY Act) could boost crypto
SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic IPOs set to test AI boom limits

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy, defense, and inflation-hedged assets (TIPS, commodities). Reduce duration in fixed income.
Avoid travel and leisure stocks with Africa exposure. Monitor healthcare stocks for vaccine/therapeutics plays. Consider Gilead or other antiviral developers.
Reduce exposure to discretionary retail and restaurants. Favor low-risk defensive names (HD, XOM, TMUS) and dividend stocks.
Short-duration bonds, floating rate notes, and value stocks. Avoid long-duration growth stocks with high valuations.
Take partial profits on high-flying AI stocks (ARM, Nvidia). Rotate into AI infrastructure and energy plays with more tangible earnings.
Hedge EM exposure with currency forwards or reduce allocation. Focus on countries with strong fundamentals (Bangladesh fund) or specific catalysts (Alibaba).
Monitor CLARITY Act progress; consider small Bitcoin allocation if bill passes. Stay diversified across regions.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and energy stocks on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: AI demand is structural; Vertiv, ASML, and AI energy stocks (VRT, ASML) have strong momentum and near buy points. ARM is at all-time highs but wait for pullback.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate airline stocks with strong fundamentals and consolidation benefits
opportunities.rationale: Ryanair record profits, Allegiant-Sun Country acquisition, JetBlue-United partnership. Low-cost carriers and network airlines with loyalty programs are well-positioned despite fuel costs.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in healthcare leaders with strong pipelines (obesity, oncology)
opportunities.rationale: Eli Lilly's retatrutide positive Phase 3 and Merck's endometrial cancer trial success reinforce growth. Obesity and oncology are multi-year themes.

opportunities.recommendation: Add defense and space stocks on government contract wins
opportunities.rationale: Space Force awards, SpaceX test success, and L3Harris ramp provide catalysts. Defense spending likely to increase amid geopolitical tensions.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider cybersecurity stocks for rapid growth through 2028
opportunities.rationale: Headline 15 highlights cheap cybersecurity stocks poised for growth. AI and cloud adoption drive demand.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and long-duration growth stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: High gas prices, food costs, and retail closures signal consumer strain. Bond yields staying high pressure growth valuations.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to dividend stocks in tax-advantaged accounts (Roth IRA)
defensive_moves.rationale: Headline 47 highlights dividend stocks generating tax-free income. Defensive dividends provide income amid uncertainty.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against inflation with TIPS, commodities, and energy stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: War-driven inflation persists; Fed's favored gauge shows price pressures. Energy stocks (Kosmos, Frontline, Exxon) benefit from elevated prices.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor Ebola outbreak and reduce travel exposure if it worsens
defensive_moves.rationale: Ebola spreading in Congo/Uganda with US aid down 99%. Could disrupt travel and supply chains. Avoid airlines with Africa routes.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain range-bound with elevated volatility due to Iran conflict, Fed policy uncertainty, and Ebola outbreak. S&P 500 and Nasdaq may test recent highs but face headwinds from bond yields and inflation. Consumer discretionary and growth stocks could underperform. Energy, defense, and healthcare may outperform.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: If Iran conflict de-escalates, bond yields could fall, boosting growth stocks. AI infrastructure and cybersecurity remain secular growth themes. Obesity and oncology drugs offer long-term healthcare growth. Emerging markets may recover if political risks subside. Bitcoin could rally if CLARITY Act passes. SpaceX IPO could be a major event.
key_catalysts: Fed Chair Warsh's first policy decision and commentary (headlines 6, 41)
SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic IPO filings and pricing (headline 28)
CLARITY Act progress and Bitcoin regulatory clarity (headline 73)
Q2 earnings season and corporate guidance (headlines 1, 4, 5, 9, 10, 16, 35, 38, 53, 60, 63, 65, 67, 78, 85, 94, 99)
ECB and other central bank policy responses to inflation (headline 24)
US presidential trade and tariff announcements (headlines 11, 14)
watch_list: Iran nuclear deal negotiations and Hormuz shipping traffic
Fed Chair Warsh speeches and FOMC minutes
Ebola case counts and travel advisories
AI infrastructure capex announcements from Meta, Google, Microsoft
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
Oil and gas prices (Brent, WTI, Henry Hub)
SpaceX and other high-profile IPO progress
Emerging market currency and bond spreads
